description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular array of dark photovoltaic panels mounted on a low-profile base. The panels are set within a metallic frame, likely aluminum, and are slightly elevated above the base, which appears to house electrical components or support structures. The base is primarily gray with some red and black detailing, possibly indicating electrical access points or safety markings. The surface of the panels is smooth and reflective, typical of tempered glass used in solar modules. There are no visible moving parts, and the structure appears designed for ground or flat-roof installation in urban environments. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for integration into public spaces or utility areas.
description: A compact, rectangular solar panel array with a metallic frame and a low-profile base, featuring dark photovoltaic surfaces and subtle red and black detailing.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full width of the solar panel array and the base, with clear visibility of the dark panels and red detailing on the lower edge.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thickness of the base and the slight elevation of the panels above it, with some structural details visible on the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the base and the continuation of the panel array, with less visible detailing compared to the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the panel elevation and the base's structure, mirroring features seen in view 1 but from the other side.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 10 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a low-profile aluminum frame
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 2-3 rectangular panels arranged side by side
